description_long: This traffic barricade is a portable, modular barrier commonly used for crowd control or to block off restricted areas in urban environments. The main body is constructed from a white, durable plastic or composite material, featuring five vertical rectangular cutouts for reduced weight and wind resistance. Bright orange reflective stripes are horizontally positioned along the top and bottom, enhancing visibility in low-light conditions. The barricade stands on two wide, black support legs that provide stability and prevent tipping. The ends of the barricade have molded features, including hooks or slots, allowing multiple units to be connected in series. There are no visible lights or warning signage, and the surface appears smooth and hard.
description: A white modular traffic barricade with orange reflective stripes and two wide black support legs, designed for crowd or traffic control. It features vertical cutouts and connection points for linking multiple units.
description_view_0: Front view showing five vertical cutouts, orange reflective stripes, and both support legs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the barricade and the triangular shape of the support legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid back panel, support legs, and absence of reflective stripes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the narrow edge and support leg structure.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Horizontal orange stripes, rectangular, evenly spaced along the top and bottom front face.
